source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Thunder Horse Platform Pollution Event

context.region: Gulf of Mexico - Mississippi Canyon Block 778

event.top_event: Loss of Containment - Produced Water Discharge
actions_taken: BSEE Accident Investigator sent email requesting documents on 6 May 2021
BSEE AI and two inspectors flew to Thunder Horse platform on 14 May 2021
Documents and produced water system drawings were collected
Investigation conducted and incident report submitted by BP on 21 May 2021
event.summary: A pollution event occurred on 4 May 2021 at Thunder Horse platform (MC 778 A) when approximately 5 barrels of hydrocarbons were released into the Gulf of Mexico from the produced water outfall. The incident was caused by operator error leaving the water clarifier injection quill to Float Cell #2 open, allowing 286 gallons of water clarifier to gravity drain into the vessel. This excess clarifier caused a lack of oil/water separation. The issue was prolonged because a faulty level control valve in Float Cell #1 prevented switching to the backup produced water train. The sheen was first detected via satellite imagery on 4 May 2021 and the discharge continued through 9 May 2021.

hazards.name: Water clarifier chemical in produced water system

threats.name: Operator leaves water clarifier injection quill open
Level control valve failure in Float Cell #1
Excess water clarifier ingress into Float Cell #2

consequences.name: Discharge of hydrocarbons to Gulf of Mexico

controls.name: Produced water treatment system - Float Cell design
controls.barrier_role: Prevent excess clarifier chemical from entering produced water discharge

supporting_text: During a shut down, the water clarifier injection quill to Float Cell #2 was left open which gravity drained 286 gallons of water clarifier into the Float Cell #2

controls.name: Level control valve in Float Cell #1
controls.barrier_role: Enable switchover from Float Cell #2 to Float Cell #1 to restore oil/water separation

supporting_text: a broken level control valve on the Float Cell #1 prevented the switch over from Float Cell #2
This separation issue was prolonged due to a level control valve failure in Float Cell #1 which did not allow BP to switch from Float Cell #2 to Float Cell #1

controls.name: Oil/water separation process in produced water treatment
controls.barrier_role: Separate oil from water before discharge to prevent hydrocarbon release

supporting_text: The excess water clarifier caused a lack of water/oil separation and oily water went out the overboard water discharge
a buildup of water clarifier in Float Cell #2 created a lack of oil/water separation within the produced water discharge system

controls.name: Operator task procedures and work instructions
controls.barrier_role: Ensure proper closure of water clarifier injection quill during shutdown

supporting_text: Human Performance Error – Inattention to task: The water clarifier injection quill to Float Cell #2 was left open
The produced water issues were first caused by an operator error which allowed an abnormal amount of water clarifier to ingress into the produced water vessels

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
